title: Obligations of Data Fiduciary
description: Every Data Fiduciary shall make reasonable efforts to ensure personal data processed is accurate and complete, implement appropriate security safeguards, and notify the Board of any data breach.
compliance_requirements: Appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO)
Implement security safeguards (ISO 27001 or equivalent)
Report breaches to Data Protection Board within 72 hours
Maintain accuracy and completeness of personal data

title: Notice regarding personal data processing
description: Data Fiduciaries must provide a clear, accessible notice to Data Principals before processing personal data, describing the purpose, categories, basis, and rights available.
compliance_requirements: Provide notice in clear and plain language
Specify purpose of processing and categories of data
Specify legal basis for processing
Inform Data Principals of their rights under the Act
Notice must be available in English and all major Indian languages

title: Grounds for processing personal data
description: Personal data may be processed only for a lawful purpose with the consent of the Data Principal, or for certain legitimate uses such as employment, legal compliance, medical emergency, or public service.
compliance_requirements: Obtain free, specific, informed, unconditional consent with unambiguous indication
Provide ability to withdraw consent at any time
Document legitimate use basis where consent is not relied upon
Ensure processing is for a lawful purpose

title: Deemed consent
description: Processing permitted without explicit consent for specified grounds including: legal obligation, public function, medical emergency, employment-related processing, or when consent is not reasonably practicable.
compliance_requirements: Document the deemed consent ground being relied upon
Ensure processing is strictly necessary for the claimed purpose
Maintain records of why explicit consent was not obtained
Notify Data Principals of deemed consent processing

title: Obligation of Data Principal
description: Data Principals shall not impersonate another person, suppress material information, or make false or frivolous complaints to the Board.
compliance_requirements: Implement identity verification for data principal requests
Maintain audit logs of complaints and requests
Flag frivolous or repeated requests

title: Rights of Data Principal
description: Data Principals have rights to: confirmation of processing, access, correction, erasure, portability, grievance redressal, and the right to nominate a representative.
compliance_requirements: Implement processes for right to confirmation and access
Implement correction and erasure workflows
Provide data portability in machine-readable format (CSV, JSON)
Establish a grievance redressal mechanism with 30-day resolution timeline
Allow nomination of representative

title: Grievance Redressal by Data Fiduciary
description: Data Fiduciaries must establish an effective mechanism to address complaints from Data Principals, with a response within 30 days from the date of complaint.
compliance_requirements: Establish a grievance officer contactable via multiple channels
Acknowledge complaints within 24 hours
Resolve complaints within 30 days
Maintain a grievance register with resolution status
Publish grievance officer contact details on website

title: Transfer of personal data outside India
description: Personal data may be transferred outside India to countries or territories notified by the Central Government, subject to standard contractual clauses or other approved safeguards.
compliance_requirements: Transfer only to notified countries or territories
Execute standard contractual clauses (SCCs) with recipients
Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) before transfer
Maintain record of all cross-border data transfers
Notify Data Protection Board of significant transfers

title: Exemptions for processing of personal data of children
description: Processing of personal data of children is prohibited where profiling, tracking, or behavioural monitoring is involved, or where processing would cause harm. Verifiable parental consent is required.
compliance_requirements: Implement age verification mechanisms
Obtain verifiable parental consent for child data processing
Prohibit profiling, tracking, and behavioural monitoring of children
Prohibit processing likely to cause harm to children
Classify as Significant Data Fiduciary if processing child data at scale

title: Obligations of Significant Data Fiduciary
description: Additional obligations for entities classified as Significant Data Fiduciaries by the Board, based on volume of data, sensitivity, risk to rights, and turnover.
compliance_requirements: Appoint an independent Data Auditor
Conduct annual Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIA)
Conduct periodic audits of processing activities
Appoint a DPO based in India
Implement enhanced security measures including encryption and access controls
Register with the Data Protection Board

title: Data Protection Officer
description: The DPO must be appointed in writing, possess expert knowledge of data protection law, be based in India, serve as a point of contact, and monitor the Data Fiduciary's compliance with the Act.
compliance_requirements: Appoint DPO in writing with clearly defined responsibilities
DPO must be based in India
DPO must report directly to the highest management level
Publish DPO contact details publicly
Ensure DPO is involved in all data protection matters

title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
description: A DPIA is mandatory when processing large volumes of sensitive personal data, profiling children, using new technologies, or when processing poses high risk to Data Principals' rights.
compliance_requirements: Conduct DPIA before commencing high-risk processing
Document purpose, necessity, proportionality, and risk analysis
Identify and document mitigation measures
Consult DPO during DPIA process
Review and update DPIA at regular intervals (recommended annually)
